variable_identity.standardized_name_en: Hospital length of stay in days

immutable_core.semantic_definition: A one-row-per-hospital-admission encounter duration summary retaining hospital length of stay in days from the hospital admission start boundary to the hospital discharge boundary, rather than ICU length of stay, a patient-level longitudinal duration, or a first-day partial-window summary.
immutable_core.semantic_grain: one row per anchor-qualified summary target
immutable_core.same_name_cross_database_rule: Any database asset published as std_hospital_los_days must preserve hospital-admission encounter duration meaning under a hospital admission/discharge boundary contract and must not silently narrow to ICU stay duration or widen to subject-level follow-up time.

summary_contract.window_start_rule: start at the hospital admission timestamp
summary_contract.window_end_rule: end at the hospital discharge timestamp
summary_contract.qualifying_row_rule: one retained duration summary per hospital admission encounter when the required encounter identifiers and admission/discharge boundaries are present
summary_contract.selection_rule: one hospital admission source row maps directly to one retained duration summary row
summary_contract.no_source_row_action: do not synthesize a hospital LOS row without a source hospital admission encounter row

canonical_representation.range_role_note: Hospital LOS is semantically nonnegative, but the current MIMIC reviewed asset intentionally retains rare negative-duration source edge cases with hospital_los_validity_status rather than silently deleting them. Values outside the plausible band require review before cohort use.

external_alignment.alignment_policy: Use external terminologies as semantic anchors when reliable, but do not make them the backbone of the standard-variable system.
external_alignment.note: std_hospital_los_days is a derived encounter-duration summary whose identity is governed primarily by hospital admission/discharge boundaries, encounter grain, and day-unit representation rather than one exact external code.

new_variable_trigger_examples: changing std_hospital_los_days from hospital admission duration to ICU stay duration
changing std_hospital_los_days from encounter-level duration to subject-level follow-up time
changing std_hospital_los_days from full hospital admission duration to a first-day or partial-window duration
version_bump_trigger_examples: adding a new reviewed database mapping while preserving the same hospital-admission duration meaning
tightening the duration range guidance or validity-status handling without changing the interval meaning

allowed_database_specific_variation: raw interval fields used to define hospital admission and discharge boundaries
database-specific local structural keys that normalize into subject_id and hadm_id
presence or absence of audit-only reported LOS fields alongside the retained primary interval duration
database-specific validity-status labels when explicitly mapped to the standard validity policy
disallowed_same_name_variation: publishing ICU length of stay as std_hospital_los_days
publishing ICU/MC stay-equivalent duration as std_hospital_los_days without true hospital admission/discharge semantics
dropping negative-duration or boundary-edge cases silently without a validity-status or exclusion policy

execution_governance.formal_output_rule: Formal std_hospital_los_days outputs should go through execution.py reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec. Execute-mode runtime evidence should record both spec hashes and formal output-file signatures.
execution_governance.future_non_bypassable_rule: Formal std_hospital_los_days outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading this variable_spec and an approved mapping_spec.

not_yet_approved_database_notes: AmsterdamUMCdb-1.0.2 must be separately reviewed for true hospital-admission LOS semantics before it can share this same variable identity. ICU/MC-only duration should remain under a distinct ICU/stay-duration identity.
